description: Phase B must be Stale GH issue detection (replacing the old inbox.md task dedup phase)
expect: Phase B — Stale GH issue detection

description: Skill must NOT reference inbox.md anywhere — tasks are GH issues now. Positive marker verifies the replacement phase heading is in place.

D2 result logic must define pass to cover runs where phases completed cleanly AND Needs Review has items — the items are informational output for a living vault, not a failure signal. Prevents the prior 100% partial-rate miscalibration.

D3 must auto-invoke /improve only on result == fail (phase errored), not on any result != pass. Otherwise healthy nightly runs (Needs Review has items → partial under old rule) fire /improve wastefully with no actionable signal.

D3 must carry an inline note flagging the intentional divergence from skill-spec.md § 11 auto-improve convention (default: result != pass). Prevents a future maintainer from reverting the tightening 'back to the convention' without reading the improve report.
C3 empty grill detection must use prefix match (case-insensitive) for ## Decisions headings, not exact match. Prevents false positives for ## Decisions Made, ## Decisions (locked), etc.
A2 orphan exclusion list must include business/intelligence/emails/ — auto-generated dated intelligence artifacts with the same rationale as daily/ (dated, unlinked by design).
C3 empty grill detection must exclude files whose filename contains 'autogrill' — auto-generated planning/design sessions not required to have a formal ## Decisions section.
A2 orphan exclusion list must include Clippings/ — Obsidian web clipper artifacts are standalone by design and expected to be unlinked.

A2 orphan exclusion list must include people/, companies/, and meetings/ — auto-generated email entity records are database-style artifacts, orphaned by design (same rationale as business/intelligence/emails/). These directories consistently account for 95%+ of orphan flags. Bidirectional link gaps for entity pages are handled by Phase A3.

C3 empty grill detection must match both singular 'Decision' and plural 'Decisions' as a prefix for H2 headings — captures ## Decision Summary, ## Settled Decisions, and similar variants that encode real decisions but use non-standard heading text.

C3 empty grill detection must also match H1 '# Decision' prefix in addition to H2 '## Decision' — some sessions use H1 headings (e.g. '# Decisions' table, '# Decision Summary') which are valid decision records. Prevents false-positive flags when decisions are encoded under H1 rather than H2.

A1 broken wiki-link scan must exclude Obsidian @-mention links ([[@...]]) — these are person-mention syntax, not file references, and always appear broken in a filesystem check. Eliminates structural false positives from Obsidian @-tag usage.

D2 result logic must classify context-constrained skips (Phase A4: update-readmes.sh absent; Phase B: gh unauthenticated or gh_task_repo unset) as pass, not partial. These skips are structural in MCP-mode execution — all MCP-accessible phases still ran and the report is fully correct. Prevents persistent 50% partial rate in the improve scanner from polluting skill health metrics.

C3 empty grill detection must exclude grill-session files with status: shipped frontmatter — shipped grills are completed artifacts; their decisions are locked, not missing. Prevents false-positive flags that cause inconsistency between MCP and interactive runs.

A4 must specify explicit recovery for script execution failure (non-zero exit or corrupt output): revert uncommitted changes and treat as context-constrained skip — result pass, not partial. Prevents the 2026-05-30 Linux stat-mismatch partial.

D2 must require the result field to be the bare token pass/partial/fail, with run detail in a trailing note="..." key=value field. Prevents the 2026 Q2 drift where runs wrote 200-1000 char prose narratives into field 7, breaking every field-7 parser.

A1 must exclude daily/organize-reports/ and daily/skill-outcomes/ as link sources — lint artifacts quote broken links verbatim, so scanning them re-finds each run's own prior findings (the recurring 7k+ raw-broken explosions).

A1 must exclude business/intelligence/emails/ as link sources — auto-generated dated artifacts linking to rotated gmail-triage files, broken by design (~216 stable noise links per run before encoding).
expect: applied here to files as link sources

description: A1 must exclude [[https://...]] URL pseudo-links — URLs in wiki-link syntax are not file references and can never resolve.

A1 must strip fenced code blocks before scanning — wikilinks inside code fences are quoted examples/data, not graph edges. Encodes the code-block-aware resolver that the best local runs already applied ad hoc.
